mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments that require efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as per your requirements.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the status of your nodes as 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify that the deployment is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d --output=yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.6.0.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/llm-d-deployment

```

> Backup current deployment configurations.
> This command upgrades llm-d to the latest version.
> Verify that the deployment has been successfully updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the application is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly configured and the application is running.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced intelligent inference scheduling and workload autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state, and 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods running without errors.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
